prompt: Create a premium dark-fantasy card frame for an original game called Blood Rogue. Straight-on single card front only, portrait 2:3. Design a clean gothic bronze-and-iron attack card frame with explicit empty regions for content. The frame itself must visually define these regions with no text labels: 1) a centered title plaque in the top band sized for two lines, about 62% card width and 10% card height, 2) a large central art window with crisp visible boundaries and dark interior, about 84% card width and 48% card height, 3) a thin centered type strip directly below the art window, about 56% card width and very short height, 4) one single lower parchment writing well integrated into the frame, about 84% card width and 23% card height. Include a clear top-left cost orb seat but do not place a number. The lower writing well must be a single surface, not a parchment box inside another parchment box. Keep the frame elegant, readable, and quieter than Monster Train. Use dark iron, aged bronze, subtle ember accents, worn leather, parchment, and shadow. No text, no glyphs, no placeholder letters, no logos, no watermark, no extra boxes, no tiny decorative clutter.

Create a premium dark-fantasy card frame for an original game called Blood Rogue. Straight-on single card front only, portrait 2:3. Make a slightly more Slay the Spire inspired version of a gothic attack card frame: bold outer border, strong top plaque for the title, a clean framed art opening, a narrow label shelf under the art, and one integrated lower writing well. Keep the structure explicit and easy to align content to. Geometry should read clearly: top title plaque centered and wide enough for two lines, art window centered and bounded by a clear rim, thin type line shelf centered below it, one lower parchment panel centered beneath with no second inset box. Leave a visible seat for a cost orb in the upper left. Materials should be dark lacquered iron, old bronze trim, aged parchment, muted crimson embers. More readable and less cramped than the current frame. No text, no fake Latin, no icons, no numbers, no watermark, no extra nested text box.

Create a premium dark-fantasy card frame for an original game called Blood Rogue. Straight-on single card front only, portrait 2:3. Design a cleaner and more architectural attack card frame with the content wells clearly built into the card: a top title plaque, one large central art frame, a small centered subtitle strip, and a single wide lower rules well. Emphasize alignment and usable empty space over ornament. The title plaque should feel carved into the frame and support two lines. The art window should have an obvious boundary and should not bleed into the lower section. The type strip should float lightly between art and rules. The rules well should be a single integrated parchment field, not a card within a card. Reserve the top-left corner for a cost orb anchor. Style it as mature gothic deckbuilder UI, with blackened iron, dim brass, weathered parchment, subtle blood-red edge accents, and restrained filigree. No text, no letters, no runes, no logos, no watermark, no extra boxes or stacked panels.
